description: Supplementary regressions for topic-aware public-video discovery, attachment staging, long-video retries, exact on-screen identity, reliable inline frame rendering, honest no-vision behavior, warm-cache latency, and animated GIFs.

measurement.provenance_rule: Fail any answer attributed to Keyframe unless video_ingest returned status=ready and a video_id.

variables.LOCAL_VIDEO: A selected local recording with audio and no caption sidecar
variables.LONG_LOCAL_VIDEO: A selected local recording between 30 minutes and 4 hours with audio and no caption sidecar
variables.MOTHERBOARD_VIDEO: The public Linus Tech Tips PC-build guide at https://youtu.be/s1fxZ-VWs2U, already indexed in the warm cache
variables.EXACT_IDENTITY_VIDEO: A first-party macOS screen recording with audio that shows one issue being merely considered, then later shows a different issue while a mentor says it was fixed and merged; both issue identities are readable on screen
variables.ANIMATED_GIF: A first-party animated GIF with at least three visibly distinct states
variables.WEAK_DISCOVERY_TOPIC: A prepared niche build request for which current public results only mention adjacent technologies and do not teach the requested task

cases.prompt: Use Keyframe to summarize {{LOCAL_VIDEO}} with a timestamped timeline. If the selected file is outside authorized roots, stage only that attachment at the exact path Keyframe reports and retry once. Do not use native video analysis as a silent fallback.
success_criteria: A successful ingest receipt has status=ready and a video_id
Audio-bearing media returns has_transcript=true through captions or local Whisper
At most one successful fast ingest and one full upgrade
At most one 12-moment list and four frame retrievals
Any native fallback requires explicit user consent and is labeled

cases.prompt: Repeat the completed Keyframe ingest for {{LOCAL_VIDEO}} without refresh, then answer one cached search question using the existing video ID.
success_criteria: The same video_id is returned
cache_hit=true
The ingest tool completes in under one second
No duplicate full upgrade or native media analysis occurs

cases.prompt: Use Keyframe to summarize {{LONG_LOCAL_VIDEO}} as a timestamped whole-video timeline. Start with the default duration guard and follow any actionable retry without splitting or restaging the source.
success_criteria: A duration error causes one same-source retry changing only max_duration_s to the exact suggested value
The source is not split and a staged attachment is not copied a second time
The successful fast ingest reports request-local stage timings
Exactly one 12-moment routing list is used for the fast index generation
For a video longer than 30 minutes, video_get_transcript uses view=compact; a 115-minute source fits in one 200-block call
No generic video_search, transcript fan-out, or unbounded exact-transcript paging is used for the whole-video summary
At most two probe frames are inspected and fast coverage is not upgraded solely because the video contains a demonstration

cases.prompt: Use Keyframe to identify the exact issue the mentor fixed in {{EXACT_IDENTITY_VIDEO}}. An earlier, different on-screen issue is only being considered. Link the spoken referent to the visual title, number, and merged state in the same episode, and verify the decisive retained frame.
success_criteria: Finds the spoken anchor where the mentor's fixed work is introduced and retrieves bounded transcript context around it
Runs a channel=shown search with that episode's start_s and end_s; any moment list keeps the same bounds
Rejects the earlier merely considered issue and never joins its identity to the later fixed or merged statement
If probe coverage does not contain the decisive timestamp, first calls video_get_frame with that t and quality=auto to use a bounded local/proxy seek
Uses exactly one of moment_id or t for the decisive frame and reports evidence_quality plus actual_t
Upgrades the same video once to full only if one targeted seek cannot settle the sequence, then refreshes moment IDs
Uses no whole-transcript or all-moments bulk dump and retrieves only the few local candidates needed

If the attached image is available, inspects it; if the host reports image content omitted, labels the answer OCR-derived and corroborates it with adjacent same-window full-index OCR instead of claiming visual confirmation
Reports the later issue title or number and merged state with the decisive timestamp

cases.prompt: [@keyframe] How can I build my own processor? Are there any videos about it?
success_criteria: Treats the Keyframe invocation as capability selection and interprets processor as a CPU unless contrary conversation context exists
Uses host web search before Keyframe ingestion; topical queries contain CPU, processor-design, HDL, or FPGA terms and do not add Keyframe, keyframes, or video processor
Considers at most three individual public videos and requires the selected video's central subject and instructional task to match CPU construction
When a strong match exists, calls video_ingest exactly once with that concrete URL in fast mode and receives status=ready plus a video_id
Uses at least one bounded Keyframe evidence call with the returned video_id and cites verified timestamps in the CPU-building guidance
Attributes URL discovery to host web search and timestamped transcript or visual evidence to Keyframe
Recommends at most two additional relevant links without ingesting them

cases.prompt: [@keyframe] I mean a custom video-processing pipeline, not a CPU. Are there videos about building one?
success_criteria: Preserves the explicit video-processing-pipeline meaning rather than forcing the CPU interpretation
Uses host web search without adding Keyframe or keyframes unless a result is genuinely about Keyframe
Applies the same strong central-match gate and selects at most one direct individual-public-video watch URL for ingestion; only the existing same-source duration retry may repeat video_ingest
Does not pass a course, article, playlist, channel, search-result, or product landing page to video_ingest and does not auto-ingest an adjacent fallback after failure
Any timestamp claim follows a successful Keyframe ingest and bounded evidence retrieval

cases.prompt: [@keyframe] Find and explain a build video about {{WEAK_DISCOVERY_TOPIC}}.
success_criteria: Recognizes that keyword overlap, a passing mention, and an adjacent technology are not a strong match
Does not call video_ingest for any weak candidate merely to exercise Keyframe
Qualifies at most three candidate links and asks the user to choose or refine the topic
Does not attribute any un-ingested result or timestamp to Keyframe

cases.model: Luna 5.6 medium with host web search disabled
cases.prompt: [@keyframe] How can I build my own processor? Are there any videos about it?
success_criteria: Interprets processor as a CPU but does not pretend the local Keyframe library is public-web discovery
Does not pass a topical search phrase to video_ingest and does not call video_search as an internet substitute
States that Keyframe needs a supplied or externally discovered URL and asks for one
May provide clearly non-Keyframe general CPU-building guidance but invents no video recommendation or timestamp evidence

cases.prompt: Using Keyframe and the already indexed {{MOTHERBOARD_VIDEO}}, can you share a clear photo from the video of the demonstrated motherboard installation?
success_criteria: Uses exactly one cache-hit video_ingest to obtain the authoritative video_id, chapter map, and visual coverage without reprocessing media
Warm-cache agent wall time is under 30 seconds
Uses region=full and quality=auto, rejects the title card and spoken action announcement, and aligns the frame to narration where motherboard installation is underway or complete

For this fixture, actual_t is between 4725 and 4741 seconds and the rendered image visibly shows the motherboard installed or being installed in the case—not the presenter, the section title, or the later front-panel-header close-up
Because Spark has no image input, finishes timestamp selection first, calls video_get_frame exactly once, and never retrieves a frame merely to test whether it looks right
Pastes the selected structured render_markdown byte-for-byte, including its angle-bracket destination delimiters, so the image renders inline from render_path
The render_path bytes equal the sole MCP image block bytes
Does not load SKILL.md and makes no browser, shell/terminal command, web-download, playback, screenshot, extra-copy, or permission-request action
Never requests quality=source for the remote video
When image input is unavailable, makes the exact render_markdown its entire final response with no metadata, OCR, caveat, invitation, or unsupported visual claim

cases.prompt: Using Keyframe and the already indexed {{MOTHERBOARD_VIDEO}}, can you share a clear photo from the video of the demonstrated motherboard installation?
success_criteria: Uses exactly one cache-hit video_ingest to obtain the authoritative video_id and follows the image-capable at-most-two-distinct-frame policy

Pastes the selected render_markdown byte-for-byte, including its angle-bracket destination delimiters; does not load SKILL.md and uses no browser, shell/terminal command, download, screenshot, or permission action
The image renders inline and matches the exact sole MCP image block bytes
For this fixture, actual_t is between 4719 and 4741 seconds and the displayed frame visibly shows the motherboard installed or being installed in the case
Any accompanying visual description is based on image inspection and matches the displayed frame
Never claims that components outside the selected frame are visible

cases.prompt: Use Keyframe to explain the state sequence in {{ANIMATED_GIF}}. Verify the middle state with one source frame.
success_criteria: Ingest returns status=ready, has_audio=false, and visual_coverage=probe or full
Whisper is not invoked
A full upgrade is used only if the sparse probe cannot settle the sequence
The answer cites actual timestamps and inspects no more than four frames
